title: NodePort Service for External Access
description:
Expose a web app Deployment outside the cluster using a NodePort Service. NodePort reserves a port (30000–32767) on every cluster node, forwarding traffic to your Pods — no cloud load balancer needed.

**Why this matters**: NodePort is the simplest way to expose a service externally in bare-metal or local (minikube/kind) clusters. Understanding it also helps you grasp how LoadBalancer Services work under the hood.

**Common Pitfalls**: NodePort values outside 30000–32767 are rejected. Remember that clients must dial `<NodeIP>:<nodePort>`, not the Service ClusterIP port.
steps: Complete the Deployment: 2 replicas of nginx:alpine labeled app=webapp
Set nodePort to 30080 (must be in range 30000-32767)
Verify Service type and nodePort: kubectl get svc webapp-svc
Verify endpoints exist: kubectl get endpoints webapp-svc
hints: Set spec.type to NodePort in the Service manifest
nodePort field goes inside the ports array entry, alongside port and targetPort
If you omit nodePort, Kubernetes auto-assigns one — specify 30080 to make it predictable
In minikube use 'minikube service webapp-svc --url' to get the accessible URL

editor_config.initial_code:
# --- Web App Deployment ---
apiVersion: apps/v1
kind: Deployment
metadata:
  name: webapp
spec:
  replicas: # TODO: how many replicas?
  selector:
    matchLabels:
      app: webapp
  template:
    metadata:
      labels:
        app: webapp
    spec:
      containers:
      - name: webapp
        image: nginx:alpine
        ports:
        - containerPort: 80
---
# --- NodePort Service ---
apiVersion: v1
kind: Service
metadata:
  name: webapp-svc
spec:
  type: # TODO: which Service type exposes a port on every node?
  selector:
    app: webapp
  ports:
  - port: 80
    targetPort: 80
    nodePort: # TODO: pick a port in the 30000-32767 range

_solution.code_solution:
# --- Web App Deployment ---
apiVersion: apps/v1
kind: Deployment
metadata:
  name: webapp
spec:
  replicas: 2
  selector:
    matchLabels:
      app: webapp
  template:
    metadata:
      labels:
        app: webapp
    spec:
      containers:
      - name: webapp
        image: nginx:alpine
        ports:
        - containerPort: 80
---
# --- NodePort Service ---
apiVersion: v1
kind: Service
metadata:
  name: webapp-svc
spec:
  type: NodePort
  selector:
    app: webapp
  ports:
  - port: 80
    targetPort: 80
    nodePort: 30080
_solution.validation_script:
#!/bin/bash
set -e

kubectl apply -f solution.yaml

echo "Waiting for webapp Deployment to be ready..."
kubectl rollout status deployment/webapp --timeout=90s
if [ $? -ne 0 ]; then
  echo "✗ webapp Deployment did not become ready"
  exit 1
fi
echo "✓ webapp Deployment is ready"

SVC_TYPE=$(kubectl get svc webapp-svc -o jsonpath='{.spec.type}' 2>/dev/null || echo "")
if [ "$SVC_TYPE" != "NodePort" ]; then
  echo "✗ Service type is '$SVC_TYPE', expected 'NodePort'"
  exit 1
fi
echo "✓ Service type is NodePort"

NODE_PORT=$(kubectl get svc webapp-svc -o jsonpath='{.spec.ports[0].nodePort}' 2>/dev/null || echo "")
if [ -z "$NODE_PORT" ]; then
  echo "✗ Service webapp-svc has no nodePort assigned"
  exit 1
fi
echo "✓ NodePort assigned: $NODE_PORT"

if [ "$NODE_PORT" != "30080" ]; then
  echo "✗ Expected nodePort 30080, got $NODE_PORT"
  exit 1
fi
echo "✓ nodePort is correctly set to 30080"

ENDPOINTS=$(kubectl get endpoints webapp-svc -o jsonpath='{.subsets[0].addresses}' 2>/dev/null || echo "")
if [ -z "$ENDPOINTS" ] || [ "$ENDPOINTS" = "null" ]; then
  echo "✗ Service webapp-svc has no endpoints"
  exit 1
fi
echo "✓ Service webapp-svc has endpoints"

REPLICAS=$(kubectl get deployment webapp -o jsonpath='{.status.readyReplicas}' 2>/dev/null || echo "0")
if [ "$REPLICAS" != "2" ]; then
  echo "✗ Expected 2 ready replicas, got $REPLICAS"
  exit 1
fi
echo "✓ Deployment has 2 ready replicas"

echo "✓ NodePort Service validation passed"
exit 0
